quotes.text: People think being an astronaut is about being fearless. It's actually about being afraid of exactly the right things at exactly the right time.
quotes.speaker: Chris Hadfield
quotes.context: Royal Ontario Museum public lecture series 'Ideas That Matter,' Toronto, February 2020
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Hadfield's whole public brand is reframing fear as a useful tool, and he speaks in precisely this kind of clean, aphoristic style. The giveaway is that it's a touch too tidy; his real lines tend to come wrapped in a specific mission anecdote.

quotes.text: I've cooked for kings and I've cooked for line cooks at two in the morning, and I'll tell you honestly — the line cooks are harder to impress.
quotes.speaker: Gordon Ramsay
quotes.context: Interview with Bon Appétit magazine, published in the November 2017 issue
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Gordon has a well-documented respect for working kitchen staff and frequently contrasts fine dining with the rough, honest world of professional cooks. The slight tell is 'honestly,' which he uses constantly in real interviews, making it feel almost too on-brand.

quotes.text: I don't watch my old fights anymore. What's the point? That guy didn't know what he didn't know. I'm more interested in the guy I'm going to be next year.
quotes.speaker: Floyd Mayweather Jr.
quotes.context: Interview with ESPN's Stephen A. Smith on First Take, March 2018
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Floyd genuinely talks this way about self-improvement and legacy, and the forward-looking humility fits his post-retirement persona perfectly. The subtle giveaway is the phrase 'didn't know what he didn't know,' which is slightly more introspective than Floyd typically goes.
